title: ovilla perennis
intro: Ovilla perennis, also known as American Elm, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its broad, vase-shaped canopy and distinctive bark with diamond-shaped furrows. The medicinal properties of Ovilla perennis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of using Ovilla perennis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Ovilla perennis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in poultices and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
